title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Eval Recall
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an evidence-first debug and review playbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
Rank competing hypotheses and choose discriminating checks.
Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reproduction · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Reproduction without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.

content.text: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Reproduction, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an evidence-first debug and review playbook.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The first plausible cause should be fixed immediately. This fails the Reproduction contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Adversarial Cases trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test prompts and code against misleading context, malformed inputs, and instruction injection.

content.text: For Adversarial Cases, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Adversarial Cases case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Normal examples predict behavior under adversarial input.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test prompts and code against misleading context, malformed inputs, and instruction injection. Artifact under review: an evidence-first debug and review playbook.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Adversarial Cases, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Adversarial Cases applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test prompts and code against misleading context, malformed inputs, and instruction injection.

hints: Name the Adversarial Cases precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Normal examples predict behavior under adversarial input.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and prompts plus specific case evidence.
